subject: Public Health
title: John Snow and the Birth of Epidemiology
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: John Snow and the Birth of Epidemiology.

hook.text: In 1854, a London doctor mapped cholera deaths and traced them to a single water pump—before germ theory existed.

buildup.text: John Snow plotted each death on a map and noticed they clustered around the Broad Street pump in Soho.

discovery.text: He convinced authorities to remove the pump handle—and cholera cases plummeted in the area.

twist.text: Snow's findings were dismissed for years—the medical establishment clung to the 'miasma' theory of bad air.

climax.text: Snow's method—mapping disease, forming hypotheses, and testing them—became the foundation of modern epidemiology.

punchline.text: One map, one pump handle, one doctor—and a science was born.

quiz.question: What did John Snow map to trace the cholera source?
options: Cholera deaths around a water pump
